payload.title: Unified Engine: list-item-misuse — list-item-misuse
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `list-item-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/list-item-misuse`, `/engine/list-item-misuse_success`, `/engine/list-item-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/list-item-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Fixtures:** 9 pass, 5 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `list-item-misuse` examples published at `/engine/list-item-misuse_success` and `/engine/list-item-misuse_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 9 pass, 5 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

steps.action:
Action:
1. **Step 1 — Open rule detail page in RAIDEN**
2. Navigate to `/engine/list-item-misuse` in the Engine Rules Library.
3. Verify rule title, description, WCAG references, and impact (`medium`) are displayed.
4. Confirm navigation breadcrumbs show Engine Rules → list-item-misuse.
steps.expected_result:
Expected Result:
1. Rule detail page loads without errors.
2. Title and description match engine-rules metadata.
3. WCAG references and remediation advice are visible when present.
steps.data:
Data:
BASE_URL/engine/list-item-misuse
ruleId = list-item-misuse
impact = medium

steps.action:

3. For each pass fixture listed in Data, locate the matching HTML example card.
4. Verify each example is labeled compliant and HTML matches the atomic fixture.
steps.expected_result:

2. Each pass fixture from atomic-tests/pass/ is represented as an example.
3. Example HTML content matches the corresponding atomic test file.

steps.action:

2. For each fixture in Data, run the core-engine atomic test harness.
3. Assert `response.passed === true` for pass fixtures and `false` for fail fixtures.
4. Verify passedNodes/failedNodes match testconfig expect blocks.

2. All fail fixtures return response.passed = false.
3. Node-level expect.passedNodes / expect.failedNodes assertions pass.

payload.title: Unified Engine: list-not-empty — Lists should contain at least one list item
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `list-not-empty`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/list-not-empty`, `/engine/list-not-empty_success`, `/engine/list-not-empty_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/list-not-empty/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** An empty list will still be announced by screen readers, which may confuse users, leaving them unsure if the list is empty or an issue prevents the screen reader from announcing the list items.
**Remediation advice:** Remove the empty HTML list elements (<ul>/<ol>) or assign aria-hidden="true" to make sure they are ignored by screen readers.
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 2 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `list-not-empty` examples published at `/engine/list-not-empty_success` and `/engine/list-not-empty_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 6 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: main-navigation-discernible — Main navigation should be labelled properly
payload.description:

**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/main-navigation-discernible`, `/engine/main-navigation-discernible_success`, `/engine/main-navigation-discernible_failure`

---

payload.title: Unified Engine: main-navigation-mismatch — Main navigation should have role navigation
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `main-navigation-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/main-navigation-mismatch`, `/engine/main-navigation-mismatch_success`, `/engine/main-navigation-mismatch_failure`

Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.

**Rule description:** Main navigation elements should have role navigation to ensure that screen readers can identify them as navigation regions.
**Remediation advice:** Add role=navigation to the main navigation elements

payload.title: Unified Engine: map-area-discernible — Each area element within an image map should have a text alternative
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `map-area-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/map-area-discernible`, `/engine/map-area-discernible_success`, `/engine/map-area-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/map-area-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Providing a text alternative for each area element inside a map element ensures that the content is announced by screen readers, enabling users to understand and navigate the content.
**Remediation advice:** Provide a text alternative to each <area> element using the alt attribute.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 2 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `map-area-discernible` examples published at `/engine/map-area-discernible_success` and `/engine/map-area-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 4 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.

2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.
